channel.title: Thesis on Akhil Nasser
channel.description: Recent content in Thesis on Akhil Nasser

item.title: Project 1: Master Thesis : Auto-Encoder Sparse Grids
item.description: This work was completed as part of my Master Thesis for the Computational Science program at the Technical University of Munich. Motivation Deep Learning-based approaches have become very popular across many fields due to their versatility. A popular network configuration is the Autoencoder that essentially captures the latent space representation of higher dimensional functions. The Autoencoder consists of two neural networks that work in tandem to minimize an objective function. Sparse Grids also perform a similar function as an autoencoder but whereas in Neural Networks the underlying basis functions are non-linear and intractable, in sparse grids, this is not necessarily the case.
